title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Go Client Systems · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A platform reliability team. On-call operators need a Go client that preserves redirect hops and separates transport, HTTP status, problem-detail, decode, and uncertain-write failures in one incident timeline. Planned artifact: Deliver Resilient Go Incident API Client with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Strip or deliberately reissue authorization, cookies, origin-sensitive fields, and bodies when authority changes. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Project Transport Status Domain Errors
steps.instruction:
project case — Go stakeholder SDK sends POST G-4 with authorization, then receives 302 ../moved, 307 https://upload.example.test/next, and a loop back to the first URL. Predict method, body, credential, origin, hop-budget, and SSRF decisions per step.
